docString:
When possible, `ext` lemmas are stated without a full set of arguments. As an example, for bundled
homs `f`, `g`, and `of`, `f.comp of = g.comp of ‚Üí f = g` is a better `ext` lemma than
`(‚àÄ x, f (of x) = g (of x)) ‚Üí f = g`, as the former allows a second type-specific extensionality
lemmas to be applied to `f.comp of = g.comp of`.
If the domain of `of` is `‚Ñï` or `‚Ñ§` and `of` is a `RingHom`, such a lemma could then make the goal
`f (of 1) = g (of 1)`.

For bundled morphisms, there is a `ext` lemma that always applies of the form
`(‚àÄ x, ‚áëf x = ‚áëg x) ‚Üí f = g`. When adding type-specific `ext` lemmas like the one above, we want
these to be tried first. This happens automatically since the type-specific lemmas are inevitably
defined later.

-/
def _root_.LibraryNote.¬´partially-applied_ext_lemmas¬ª : LibraryNote‚úù :=
  default‚úù
